data.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW (criterion T3 — Integration feasibility) — affirmations only, no findings.

T3 OVERLAY DISPOSITION: The plan's integrations are INTERNAL, already-used rebar capabilities, not external API/CLI/service/library calls. Per the T3 ANTI-FP clause ("an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable"), this leans not-applicable; nonetheless every named capability verifies as MATCH against the codebase, so I record those affirmations explicitly:

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria satisfied):
- [T3] technical_feasibility — The fast signature-verify claim gate is achievable as described; it already exists. `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:634-695` (`_plan_review_precheck`) implements the opt-in claim gate, and `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:66-120` (`claim_gate_check`) is the "pure local HMAC verify + a light fingerprint recompute … NO LLM call and NO network call" — directly satisfying the plan's "fast signature-verify only (p95 < ~50ms)" and "No LLM/network on the claim path" ACs.
- [T3] named subcommands/endpoints exist (MATCH): `rebar review-plan <id>` MATCHES — `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py:291,1199-1200` ("review-plan intercept") and `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:12`. MCP `run_workflow` MATCHES — `src/rebar/mcp_server.py:839`, `src/rebar/__init__.py:590`, `src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py:584`. The atomic `claim` MATCHES — `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py:331-420` (`claim_core`) is "Atomic claim … in ONE locked critical section (single commit)" with re-read under lock + ConcurrencyMismatch, matching the T9-atomicity AC's "reuse rebar's atomic `claim`". `--force` + justification MATCHES — `transition.py:668-681` audit-logs a forced bypass.
- [T3] auth/HTTPS preconditions — N/A: no external auth/HTTPS surface on the claim path. The signing dependency is a LOCAL HMAC under the environment key (`signing.verify_signature`, `src/rebar/signing.py:362`); the plan's fail-closed AC for "signing/verification machinery UNAVAILABLE" already matches `attest.py:79-80` (verify_signature exception → `{ok: False, reason: signing-unavailable}` → block), the documented fail-closed posture.
- [T3] SPIKE routing — N/A: no critical capability gap; all asserted capabilities already exist and are exercised.

No T3 findings. Clean chunk for this criterion.
